cityDNA.coreFantasy: A dense subterranean rodentfolk survival city where granaries, lantern roots, hidden markets, relief halls, patched tenements, and moon-registry courts fill every niche.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Nettleburrow is not a cave village; the uploaded images show an old survival machine with agriculture, aid, hidden trade, low housing, and official registry order built into the cavern.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Nettleburrow turns storage, redundancy, side routes, and neighbor networks into civic survival.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Rootwood, fieldstone, copper lantern cages, willow braces, limewash, burrow arches, cavern terraces, granary towers, root-lit markets, understreet vaults, scrap stalls, laundry wells, and tenement courts.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Small rodent-like beastfolk culture built around food security, route knowledge, repair, relief, and informal networks beside official records.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Roots, lanterns, grain, seeds, burrow arches, moon phase windows, laundry wells, patched tenements, scrap markets, and understreet vaults symbolize survival through redundancy.

canonLocks: Wonder: Rodentfolk Burrow-Market of the Lantern Roots.
Seat of Power: Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court.
Districts: Agricultural Terraces, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Civic Services, Slums / Informal Settlement, Low Residential.

Exact uploaded anchors include Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook, Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook, Civic Services Hall, Relief Hall, Civic Services Lantern Monument, Notice Plaza Nook, Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook, Low Residential Civic Hall, Tenement Court, Low Residential Lantern Monument, and Laundry Well Nook.
provisionalCanon: The city's highest virtue is resourcefulness.
The image pass confirms small-body scale, stacked density, lantern roots, and civic survival systems.
rejectedReads: cute rodent burrows without civic systems
hidden economy as exotic crime theater
cautionFlags: Use rodentfolk scale and low/tight routes.
Keep official and informal routes visible together.

environment.environmentalLogic: The uploaded images make underground space into agriculture, housing, market, governance, repair, and survival infrastructure.

forms: Rodentfolk Burrow-Market of the Lantern Roots
Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court
skyline: cavern ceiling and arches replace open sky
Burrow-Market of Lantern Roots as wonder heart
Terrace Granary Beacon as food signal
Moon Phase Windows Court as authority

civilization.people: Rodentfolk citizens are busy, watchful, practical, and socially interconnected; the images emphasize bundles, tight routes, food stores, relief queues, and repair culture.
civilization.governance: Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court controls names, ration rolls, relief coordination, and legal status while informal systems keep people alive.
civilization.religion: Moon phases, roots, seeds, lantern tending, and preservation rites carry subtle survival-cycle spirituality.

streets.appearance: Tunnels open into courts and terraces packed with small bodies, carts, signs, lanterns, root clusters, laundry wells, and practical movement.
streets.movement: Movement follows tunnels, stairs, ledges, crawl passages, low arches, side-stairs, and market platforms.
streets.lighting: Warm lanterns, root light, market lamps, moon windows, and blue-green civic lamps structure the city.
streets.weatherEffects: Surface rain drips through shafts and makes drainage and lantern maintenance urgent.
streets.dayCycle: Granary checks, seed halls, market setup, and relief queues dominate the day.
streets.nightCycle: Official plazas stay lit while side tunnels become selective and coded.

housing.wealthy: Registry- or granary-adjacent stacked residences with ventilation and storage.
housing.middle: Compact tunnel-connected homes and market apartments with food niches.
housing.poor: Patched tenements, low burrow rooms, shared laundry wells, and crowded stair courts.

districts.name: Agricultural Terraces
districts.civicPurpose: Food security, seed preservation, and Beacon readiness.
districts.visualIdentity: Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook.

districts.name: Underworld / Hidden Economy
districts.civicPurpose: Shadow trade, hidden storage, favors, contraband, and unofficial routes.
districts.visualIdentity: Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook.

districts.name: Civic Services
districts.civicPurpose: Relief infrastructure, notices, public aid, and records.
districts.visualIdentity: Civic Services Hall, Relief Hall, Civic Services Lantern Monument, Notice Plaza Nook.

districts.name: Slums / Informal Settlement
districts.civicPurpose: Patchwork survival, repair culture, and visible poverty outside clean registry order.
districts.visualIdentity: Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook.

districts.name: Low Residential
districts.civicPurpose: Crowded domestic life, laundry, shared water, gossip, and practical social networks.
districts.visualIdentity: Low Residential Civic Hall, Tenement Court, Low Residential Lantern Monument, Laundry Well Nook.

wonder.name: Rodentfolk Burrow-Market of the Lantern Roots
wonder.symbolicRole: Trade, roots, lanterns, burrow life, food security, and informal knowledge fused.
wonder.visualRole: Root-lit cavern market with stacked platforms, lanterns, burrow arches, bridges, and grain counters.
wonder.civicRole: Market trade, rumor exchange, food distribution, lantern maintenance, and festival gathering.
storyHooks: A rumor in the Lantern Roots reaches the city before the Registry can certify truth.

beacon.name: Terrace Granary Beacon
beacon.visualRole: Granary-linked beacon among terraces, seed halls, and hanging gardens.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates grain counting, seed storage, public warning, and relief response.
storyHooks: A Beacon change makes the city check its stores before its doors.

seatOfPower.name: Moon Phase Windows Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Registration, cycles, ration authority, and civic obligation.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Underground court with moon phase windows, sealed archives, registry halls, and terraces.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Registration, ration certification, identity disputes, relief coordination, and petitions.
storyHooks: A missing name still survives in every market route.

majorLandmarks.name: Black Market Arcade
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Unofficial systems that keep people alive beside law.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Coded arcade with hidden vaults, mist veils, lantern stalls.
storyHooks: A market ledger is more accurate than the ration roll.

majorLandmarks.name: Relief Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Relief hall with notice plaza, queues, counters, and blue-green lamps.
storyHooks: A relief notice changes which family the city admits exists.

majorLandmarks.name: Scrap Market
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Patched stalls, scrap heaps, lantern monument, repaired tools.
storyHooks: A repaired hinge reveals a route the registry missed.

majorLandmarks.name: Tenement Court
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Laundry well, lantern monument, tight homes, shared stairs.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Housing, gossip, shared water, and neighbor networks.
storyHooks: A laundry-well complaint outruns an official notice.

mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep Burrow-Market, Terrace Granary Beacon, and Moon Phase Windows Court legible together.
Use root-lit caverns, granaries, hidden arcades, relief halls, scrap markets, tenements, and laundry wells.
